item.title: Two pilots killed after Air Canada jet crashes into fire truck at LaGuardia Airport
item.description: Jazz Aviation flight originated from Montreal and was carrying 72 passengers and four crew members

item.description: Traders bet on four Bank of England rate rises this year

item.title: BlackRock’s Fink warns AI may intensify wealth inequality
item.description: CEO of world’s biggest asset manager says rich backers of artificial intelligence could reap most of the rewards

item.description: Investment in non-life insurer marks new Japan venture for Warren Buffett’s conglomerate

item.title: BKR Capital raises $20-million to finance Black entrepreneurs
item.description: Venture-capital firm completes initial fundraising round for second fund, with $50-million target for end of year

item.description: Energy security is important, yes. Here’s what Canada really needs to do to secure its future

item.description: Passenger jet flying from Montreal collided with ground vehicle

item.title: Danone to buy meal-in-bottle maker Huel in €1bn deal
item.description: French group swoops on fast-growing UK food and drink company to push deeper into ‘complete nutrition’ market

item.title: China battery trio gain $70bn as Iran war sparks ‘paradigm shift’
item.description: Share price rises for clean energy companies outstrip oil majors as investors bet on switch to renewables

item.title: Qatar Airways parks long-haul jets in storage in Spain
item.description: Transfer of planes to Teruel airport signals airline is preparing for ongoing Gulf conflict

item.title: America keeps bailing out Trump
item.description: The country’s underlying strengths are absorbing his erratic choices, including the war on Iran

item.title: Iran war is a risk to the flow of Gulf funds around the globe
item.description: World has come to depend on capital from the region more deeply than many realise

item.title: The Shakespearean animus of Botín and Orcel
item.description: The Italian’s bid for Commerzbank may prove to be the final act in European banking’s rivalrous drama

item.title: BNP Paribas surges up UK M&A rankings
item.description: French bank boosted by deals including £9.9bn Schroders takeover

item.title: UK home insurers to lose money on underwriting in 2026, analysis predicts
item.description: Higher fuel and energy costs, along with wage and price pressures, will drive up claims costs, EY says

item.title: Oceans take in a lot of heat as Earth’s energy imbalance hits record
item.description: Rate of solar radiation stored in the planet has accelerated over past two decades

item.title: ‘It’s always Putin’ behind every EU migration crisis, official says
item.description: Russian president is ‘biggest driver’ of immigration due to his role in wars over past decade, says EU commissioner

item.title: UK energy supplier flags growing debt burden as Iran conflict drives up prices
item.description: EDF says one in eight customers are already more than 45 days overdue on bills

item.title: The ghost bankers haunting the City
item.description: Unable to leave the financial district, some senior staff appear to be working long after handing in their pass

item.title: Can David Ellison pull off Hollywood’s megamerger?
item.description: The audacious deal to combine Paramount and Warner Bros has huge repercussions and steep challenges

item.title: Military briefing: the high cost of using fighters to down Iranian drones
item.description: Gulf states’ reliance on jets to battle waves of cheap Shaheds is testing their finances, pilots and planes

item.title: Solo investor Air Street raises $232mn to chase hot AI bets
item.description: London-based Nathan Benaich’s approach uses speed and focus to find a foothold and deliver returns

item.title: Senegal tapped €650mn of undisclosed borrowing in bid to avoid default
item.description: West African nation agreed total return swaps with AFC and FAB months after separate hidden debt scandal came to light

item.title: Montreal to host 18 countries for defence bank negotiations beginning Monday
item.description: In its final form, the institution could include as many as 40 countries, all NATO members or their allies

item.title: Democratizing finance has delivered both progress and harm
item.description: There is enough evidence to argue that the democratization of investing can sometimes benefit the industry at the expense of many retail investors

item.title: Iran war leaves US oil and gas dealmaking ‘in paralysis’
item.description: Surging energy prices have made it challenging to calculate transaction valuations

item.title: Real estate developers increase perks for potential homebuyers as sales slump
item.description: From steep discounts to a year of free mortgage payments, builders bet promotions will get skeptics off the fence

item.title: Young adults can’t afford homes to raise families, and city governments are at fault
item.description: tk Development charges are adding significant costs to purchasing newly built homes

item.title: Indigenous program to protect Canadian wilderness aims to bolster First Nations’ economies
item.description: First 30x30 Canada was set up to help country preserve 30% of land, 30% of water under UN agreement

item.title: If your kids can’t find a job this summer, create one
item.description: Summer jobs are hard to come by but jobs around the house such as lawn mowing and raking are easy enough for kids to pick up

item.title: Newly discovered photos show astronaut Neil Armstrong after Gemini 8 emergency landing
item.description: Previously unreleased photos show new angles of Armstrong and fellow astronaut David Scott after a splashdown off Okinawa, Japan

item.description: Plus, Teck Resources’ Fourmile gold project could be worth billions

item.title: How HR could solve Canada’s non-profit workforce crisis
item.description: According to the 2025 Changemaker Wellbeing Index, even modest investments in basic HR structure could significantly boost employee advocacy across the non-profit sector

item.title: How the Iran war could derail the AI boom
item.description: The entire chip supply chain depends on energy and chemical imports from the Middle East

item.description: Market Questions is the FT’s guide to the week ahead

item.title: Iran, U.S. trade threats after Trump demands reopening of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President warns of attacks on Iranian power plants if strait isn’t reopened in 48 hours; Iran and Hezbollah step up attacks on Israel

item.description: This will have economic consequences for the places they leave and their destinations

item.description: Whatever happens, high prices will outlive the Iran war

item.description: Market Financial Solutions was complying with rules, law firm reported to UK regulator

item.title: World faces gas supply cliff edge as Gulf’s final LNG shipments approach ports
item.description: Carriers that departed from the Middle East before Iran’s missile attacks began are due to arrive in the next 10 days

item.title: Reading Socrates in Silicon Valley
item.description: Self-proclaimed stoics who denounce self-examination only prove the bankruptcy of the tech bro worldview

item.title: Chinese IPOs in US falter amid scrutiny of manipulation schemes
item.description: Regulators crack down after trading in ‘toxic’ small-cap stocks from China inflicted losses on US investors

item.title: Federal departments and agencies to cut 12,000 full-time equivalent positions over three years
item.description: Expected cuts include 1,793 jobs at Public Services and Procurement Canada, 900 at Statistics Canada and 942 at Health Canada

item.description: US president threatens to strike Iran’s power plants if the waterway is not opened for shipping

item.description: Rising crude price will boost export revenue as Mark Carney seeks to counter Donald Trump’s trade wars

item.title: US fertiliser bosses cash in as Iran war boosts shares
item.description: CF Industries aided by access to low-cost US natural gas as energy crisis slams Asian and European competitors

item.title: Lowering the Old Age Security income threshold is no longer politically taboo
item.description: Canadians are on board with calls to redirect retirement income subsidies to tackle affordability

item.title: Chart of the Week: Global rates show deeper war fears
item.description: Investors are becoming increasingly hawkish

item.title: Life insurance sales surge in wake of Reeves’ inheritance tax shake-up
item.description: Chancellor’s targeting of pensions prompts more people to take out insurance, say industry figures Tax

item.title: Iran lets grain ships through Hormuz to shore up food supply
item.description: Agriculture has been a ‘pain point’ for Tehran after it imposes de facto blockade of waterway in response to US-Israeli war

item.title: HMRC ‘unlikely to be lenient’ with tax exiles fleeing Dubai, advisers warn
item.description: Returning Britons could face hit from both income tax and capital gains tax Tax

item.title: Is it ever right to give one child more money than another?
item.description: When it comes to gifts or inheritance, parents must decide whether to give equally, or help those in most need Tax

item.title: How the oil shock could lead to wider economic pain for Canada
item.description: Financial markets are betting that the Bank of Canada will hike interest rates three times this year, starting in July

item.title: Big drugmakers saved at least $5bn on US taxes shifting income overseas
item.description: New disclosures show how pharma groups reduced federal levies last year by assigning profits to low-tax jurisdictions Tax

item.title: Endless war and global recession: Can you feel the looming dread?
item.description: A prolonged Gulf standoff could yet deliver a far sharper shock to prices and economic growth

item.title: Kraft Heinz to invest $250-million in Montreal factory to boost capacity amid increased demand
item.description: American food giant is upgrading its 70-year-old plant after having turned around its Canadian unit

item.title: Canadian retail sales up 1.1% to $70.7-billion in January
item.description: Increase led by sales at motor vehicle and parts dealers

item.title: Iberian blackout prompts calls for Europe-wide grid reforms
item.description: Report into last year’s catastrophic outage says the problem did not lie with solar and wind power
